overview.description: Alphabet Inc. offers various products and platforms in the United States, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, the Asia-Pacific, Canada, and Latin America. It operates through Google Services, Google Cloud, and Other Bets segments. The Google Services segment provides products and services, including ads, Android, Chrome, devices, Gmail, Google Drive, Google Maps, Google Photos, Google Play, Search, and YouTube. It is also involved in the sale of apps and in-app purchases and digital content in Google Play and YouTube; and devices, as well as the p...
overview.financialIntro: In 2025, Alphabet's revenue was $402.84 billion, an increase of 15.09% compared to the previous year's $350.02 billion. Earnings were $132.17 billion, an increase of 32.01%.
overview.analystIntro: According to 65 analysts, the average rating for GOOGL stock is "Strong Buy." The 12-month stock price target is $433.55, which is an increase of 26.70% from the latest price.

data.title: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
data.text: Alphabet investors have very publicly worried that the company's massive AI spending isn't worth the money. With the company's latest earnings report, those investors should be able to relax a little.

data.title: Google's Gemini app nips at ChatGPT's heels as it nears 1 billion users
data.text: Google said its Gemini app now has 950 million monthly active users. It's been increasing steadily over the past year.

data.title: Alphabet Quadruples Profit to Nearly $120 Billion, Fueled by A.I. Investments
data.text: Google's parent company, which has spent heavily on artificial intelligence, also showed growth across its businesses, particularly in cloud computing.

data.title: Alphabet Earnings Call Transcript: Q2 2026
data.text: Revenue grew 24% year-over-year to $119.8B, driven by strong AI-led growth in Search, YouTube, and Cloud. Cloud revenue surged 82%, with backlog at $514B, while CapEx guidance was raised to meet demand. Margins face near-term pressure from supply constraints and infrastructure investment.
